name: va_1g_valid_fn_returns_2m_spec
code:
proof fn phi_2_va_1g_valid_fn_returns_2m_spec(va: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_2m_valid(va),
    ensures
        va_1g_valid(va) == true,
{

reason: Due to `when_used_as_spec(spec_va_2m_valid)` on va_1g_valid, calling va_1g_valid as a spec fn resolves to spec_va_2m_valid — so any 2M-valid address is reported as 1G-valid, silently weakening all 1G address validation

name: spec_va_2m_implies_spec_va_1g_mask

reason: MEM_1g_MASK zeros more bits than MEM_2m_MASK, so 1G-alignment implies 2M-alignment — if this verifies, it confirms the copy-paste bug makes va_1g_valid strictly weaker than intended (accepts 2M-aligned, not just 1G-aligned)
